Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Paris 2024 Olympics: Learn five cool things about Tatjana

Those sporting heroes, phew!

Tatjana Smith already has a gold medal at the Paris 2024 Olympics and she’s gunning for another. Learn more about the Team SA star.

Tatjana Smith, previously Schoenmaker when she broke the world record to claim gold in the 200m breaststroke event three years ago in Tokyo in2.18.95, has won gold again. This time, in the 100m event in Paris. 27-year-old Smith won the 100m silver in Japan but wasn’t to be denied in 2024, touching the wall first in1.05.28thanks to a massive surge in the final quarter of the race.

Info viaolympics.com’sofficial website.

“Focus on one’s improvement and one’s own goals rather than comparing oneself to others. If you’re constantly improving, you’ll eventually get to the top. For me, it was always about improving my time.”

Smith stands to become only the second swimmer in history after Penny Heyns in 1996 to complete the 100m and 200m breaststroke golden double at the same Olympics event. She wasn’t yet born when Heyns accomplished this historic feat. The 27-year-old contests her 200m breaststroke heat onWednesday 31 July at 11:00 SA timewith thesemi-finals going at 21:51 and 21:58.The finalis onThursday 01 August at 21:04.You know thatNando’s will be watching. Smith won this event in Tokyo with a time of2.18.95, a world record which has since been eclipsed by Russian Evgeniia Chikunova in 2023 with a time of2.17.55.Will Smith have taken that personally?
